LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS SANDIE CHEUNG, HENRY TSAI, LUNG SHING CO., LLC & LUNG SHING INC.'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED IN PART AND SUSTAINED IN PART, WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED AS TO EXHIBIT A TO THE EXTENT THAT THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE THAT A DISSOLUTION ACTION IS PENDING IN SAN MATEO COUNTY SUPERIOR COURT BETWEEN PLAINTIFF CHEUNG AND DEFENDANT CHEUNG; GRANTED AS TO EXH. B. (COMPLAINT IN THIS ACTION) AND DENIED AS TO EXHIBIT C (PURPORTED CONTEMPORANEOUS BUSINESS RECORDS THAT ARE SUBJECT TO DISPUTE AND CANNOT BE THE BASIS FOR RULING ON THE MERITS OF THE DEMURRER). AS TO THE DEMURRER TO THE ENTIRE COMPLAINT, DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT SHOWN THAT THIS LAWSUIT CONCERNS THE SAME MATTERS INVOLVED IN THE ONGOING DISSOLUTION ACTION IN SAN MATEO COUNTY. AS TO THE FIRST, THIRD, AND FOURTH CAUSES OF ACTION, DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT ESTABLISHED THAT THE FACE OF THE COMPLAINT ESTABLISHES THAT THESE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE BARRED BY THE APPLICABLE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS; THE COURT DOES NOT CONSIDER THE EVIDENCE ATTACHED AS EXHIBIT C TO THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE. AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION, CONSTRUCTIVE TRUST IS A REMEDY. PLAINTIFFS ARE GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO REPLEAD THIS CAUSE OF ACTION AS A REMEDY. AS TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION, CIVIL CONSPIRACY IS NOT A SEPARATE CAUSE OF ACTION. PLAINTIFFS ARE GIVEN LEAVE TO AMEND TO REPLEAD THIS CAUSE OF ACTION AS PART OF THE GENERAL CHARGING ALLEGATIONS OF THE COMPLAINT. TO THE EXTENT NOT OTHERWISE STATED, THE BALANCE OF THE DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. THIS ORDER IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANTS SEEKING A STAY OR TO CONSOLIDATE THIS MATTER WITH THE FAMILY LAW CASE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: TONY VAUGHN, CSR #6185

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Nov-21-2014 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-10-2014 Set For Trial On Dec-15-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-11-2014 Continued To Apr-23-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-09-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MICHAEL G.W. LEE, ESQ., 415.788.9000, FOR PLAINTIFFS DAVID CHEUNG ET AL.
BROCK LYLE, ESQ., 650.364.8200, FOR DEFENDANTS SANDIE CHEUNG ET AL.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTs SANDIE CHEUNG, HENRY TSAI, LUNG SHING CO., LLC & LUNG SHING INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS Overruled in part and sustained in part, with leave to amend. Defendants' Request for Judicial Notice is granted as to Exhibit A to the extent that the Court takes judicial notice that a dissolution action is pending in San Mateo County Superior Court between Plaintiff Cheung and Defendant Cheung; granted as to Exh. B. (complaint in this action) and denied as to Exhibit C (purported contemporaneous business records that are subject to dispute and cannot be the basis for ruling on the merits of the demurrer). As to the demurrer to the entire complaint, defendants have not shown that this lawsuit concerns the same matters involved in the ongoing dissolution action in San Mateo County. As to the first, third, and fourth causes of action, Defendants have not established that the face of the complaint establishes that these causes of action are barred by the applicable statute of limitations; the Court does not consider the evidence attached as Exhibit C to the request for judicial notice. As to the second cause of action, constructive trust is a remedy. Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to replead this cause of action as a remedy. As to the fifth cause of action, civil conspiracy is not a separate cause of action. Plaintiffs are given leave to amend to replead this cause of action as part of the general charging allegations of the complaint. To the extent not otherwise stated, the balance of the demurrer is overruled. This Order is without prejudice to Defendants seeking a stay or to consolidate this matter with the family law case. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTs SANDIE CHEUNG, HENRY TSAI, LUNG SHING CO., LLC and LUNG SHING INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued to December 10, 2013, to give the moving party the chance to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. The moving party shall provide a courtesy copy of its moving papers immediately with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. The Court has the opposition and reply papers. Judge: Ernest H.
